title: Does the 0.75 execution confidence bar sit on a real outcome step?
hypothesis: Ideas in the near-miss confidence band (0.675-0.75, the 10% margin below the execution bar) have a materially lower forward hit rate (excess move > 0) than ideas at or above 0.75. If the near-miss band's hit-rate CI overlaps the above band's CI, the bar at 0.75 is not carrying selective information at its current placement.
rationale: CALIBRATION_PLAN.md lists the confidence formula coefficients as evidence-based candidate #2 and requires >=200 structured evaluations over >=5 sessions before analysis. The unified confidence formula shipped 2026-07-04; this experiment is the pre-registered readout for when that sample exists. The 0.75 execution bar itself is on the never-loosen list: this experiment can motivate a proposal to a human, never a change.
decision_rule: If sample gates pass AND the near-miss band's hit-rate CI upper bound is below the above band's lower bound: record 'correctly selective', close the experiment, no proposal. If the intervals overlap AND the regret flag is true AND session stability shows positive-session share >= 0.6 in the near-miss band population: draft a shadow proposal (advisory threshold at 0.70 or 0.72, enforced:false) for human review per CALIBRATION_PLAN §2.4. Under no outcome does this experiment change the live 0.75 bar - that value is on the never-loosen list.
guardrails: Acceptance volume stays within the 5-15 accepted dry-run decisions/week band
No never-loosen gate fires more often than its pre-experiment baseline
The 0.75 execution bar is never edited as part of this experiment
never_loosen_ack: min_confidence 0.75 at execution is on the never-loosen list. This experiment produces evidence and, at most, a shadow-threshold proposal for explicit human approval.
